title: Path to Stationary Distribution in Regular vs. Absorbing Markov Chains
description: A visual comparison of how state probabilities evolve over time in a Regular Markov Chain (reaching a stationary distribution) versus an Absorbing Markov Chain (getting trapped in absorbing states).
relationships: π₀ multiplied by Pⁿ converges to π for large n in a Regular Chain
In an Absorbing Chain, probability flows from Transient States into Absorbing States and gets trapped
The stationary distribution π satisfies π = πP
Absorbing states have a probability of 1.0 in the long-term distribution
